_about: Generic 3 kg QuadPlane test vehicle. With backend='ap_native', ArduPilot uses its own built-in QuadPlane physics (the same plant that the official quadplane.parm PIDs were tuned against) — guaranteed stable hover, no UE physics in the loop. The mechanical/electrical fields below are ignored in ap_native mode but kept for documentation and potential future switch back to ue_physics.
_backend_doc: ap_native = ArduPilot runs its own built-in physics (--model quadplane), UE is a pure visualizer. PIDs work first-try because the plant matches official tuning. Use this for control-algorithm development and pymavlink testing. ue_physics = ArduPilot runs --model JSON and gets physics from UE's UVTOLPhysicsComponent. Use this when simulating a specific real vehicle with measured mass/aero (gokce, pasifik, etc.).

_sources.SIM_Plane.h: Wing area, span, chord, CL0, CLalpha, CD0, Oswald e, stall AoA — verbatim from ArduPilot SITL default coefficients struct
_sources.SIM_Frame.h: Default frame mass = 3.0 kg (Model struct default). QuadPlane adds ~50% for VTOL hardware → 3.0 kg total kept as a round number.
_sources.quadplane.parm: Official ArduPilot Tools/autotest/default_params/quadplane.parm — PIDs and limits in params.parm are taken verbatim from here

_doc.mass_kg: Total vehicle mass in kg — with full batteries, no payload. Used for F=ma and hover thrust calibration.
_doc.inertia_roll_kg_m2: Moment of inertia around the roll axis (X, wing-to-wing), in kg·m². Larger = slower roll response.
_doc.inertia_pitch_kg_m2: Moment of inertia around the pitch axis (Y, nose-to-tail), in kg·m². Larger = slower pitch response.
_doc.inertia_yaw_kg_m2: Moment of inertia around the yaw axis (Z, vertical), in kg·m². Usually the largest for a VTOL with wide wingspan.
_doc.arm_length_m: Distance from vehicle centre to each lift motor, in metres. For a quad: half the motor-to-motor diagonal.
_doc.wing_area_m2: Total wing reference area in m². ArduPilot SITL default: 0.45 m² (SIM_Plane.h: s = 0.45).
_doc.aspect_ratio: Wing aspect ratio = span² / wing_area = 1.88² / 0.45 = 7.85. From ArduPilot SITL: b=1.88, c=0.24.
_doc.oswald_e: Oswald efficiency factor (0–1). ArduPilot SITL default: 0.9 (SIM_Plane.h: oswald = 0.9).
_doc.mac_m: Mean Aerodynamic Chord in metres. ArduPilot SITL default: 0.24 m (SIM_Plane.h: c = 0.24).
_doc.cl0: Lift coefficient at zero angle of attack. ArduPilot SITL default: 0.56 (SIM_Plane.h: c_lift_0 = 0.56). Positive because the SITL model uses a cambered wing.
_doc.cl_alpha: Lift curve slope in 1/radian. ArduPilot SITL default: 6.9 (SIM_Plane.h: c_lift_a = 6.9). Slightly above the 2π theoretical because the SITL model includes camber effects.
_doc.cd0: Zero-lift (parasite) drag coefficient. ArduPilot SITL default: 0.1 (SIM_Plane.h: c_drag_p = 0.1).
_doc.cy_beta: Side-force derivative dCY/dBeta in 1/radian. Negative = sideslip produces restoring force. Typical: -0.3.
_doc.cm_aileron: Roll moment coefficient per radian of aileron deflection. Conservative value for a small test vehicle.
_doc.cm_elevator: Pitch moment coefficient per radian of elevator deflection. Negative = trailing-edge-down → nose-up.
_doc.cm_rudder: Yaw moment coefficient per radian of rudder deflection.
_doc.stall_aoa_deg: Stall angle of attack in degrees. ArduPilot SITL default: 27° (SIM_Plane.h: alpha_stall = 0.4712 rad). The SITL model is permissive — high stall angle keeps the aero stable across wide AoA range.
_doc.min_airspeed_ms: Airspeed below which all aerodynamic forces are suppressed (m/s). Hover and low-speed VTOL operations are below this threshold.
_doc.max_control_deflection_deg: Maximum control surface deflection angle in degrees. PWM 1000/2000 maps to ±this angle.
_doc.gravity_ms2: Gravitational acceleration in m/s². 9.81 at sea level.
_doc.air_density: Air density in kg/m³. 1.225 at sea level, 15°C.
